The Agent Command Center Enterprise Review Portal
Demo Hub Executive Room Pilot Packet Simulator Runtime Fleet Load Test Observability Pilot Room Supervisor Legal Audit

Enterprise Review Portal

The Agent Command Center

The Agent Command Center is a public stakeholder review surface for a large-scale AI operations control architecture.

It maps 47,979 registered agents across 1,777 departments, 5,331 units, and 175 families. The system includes the stakeholder demo hub, static simulator, exact registry, controlled runtime ladder, department-gated runtime surfaces, full-fleet control-plane review, load-test and recovery drills, observability wall, executive control room, enterprise pilot room, and enterprise pilot packet exporter.

Latest public milestone: MVP-68 — Enterprise Pilot Packet Exporter.

This site does not provide raw activate-all, arbitrary shell execution, arbitrary SQL, deploy execution, rollback execution, alert sending, or unrestricted automation. Runtime surfaces are gated, staged, audited, and controlled.

MVP-68 Enterprise Pilot Packet Exporter 47,979 Registered Agents 1,777 Departments Raw Activate-All Disabled Command Execution Disabled Deploy / Rollback / Alerts Disabled Enterprise Review Portal

Current Live Review Status

  • Registered agents: 47,979
  • Departments: 1,777
  • Units: 5,331
  • Families: 175
  • Latest public milestone: MVP-68
  • Enterprise Pilot Packet: available
  • Raw activate-all: disabled
  • Command execution: disabled
  • Deploy execution: disabled
  • Rollback execution: disabled
  • Alert sending: disabled
  • Shell execution: disabled
  • Arbitrary SQL: disabled

Some surfaces are static review pages. Some are controlled backend-wired status, audit, readiness, approval, or export surfaces. No page grants unrestricted runtime authority.

Start Here

Core review paths

Executive Control Room

Role-aware executive review surface for executives, operators, security/risk, technical reviewers, recruiters, and skeptical reviewers.

Executive Control Room

Enterprise Pilot Packet

Generate the shareable enterprise pilot packet with executive summary, safety boundaries, success criteria, risks, legal links, and decision support.

Enterprise Pilot Packet

Runtime Review Surfaces

Controlled runtime ladder

Runtime Foundation

Review the controls, schemas, and checklists required before live runtime activation.

Open Runtime Foundation

Runtime Kernel

Private/admin runtime evaluation with real backend persistence and approval flow.

Open Runtime Kernel

Runtime Agent Control

Supervised single-agent test only. Activate or deactivate one controlled runtime agent.

Open Runtime Agent Control

10-Agent Runtime Squad

Controlled 10-agent supervised squad with heartbeats and readiness notes.

Open Runtime Squad

100-Agent Runtime Battalion

Controlled 100-agent supervised battalion across 10 lanes.

Open Runtime Battalion

250-Agent Runtime Company

Controlled 250-agent supervised company across 25 lanes.

Open Runtime Company

500-Agent Runtime Group

Controlled 500-agent supervised group across 50 lanes.

Open Runtime Group

1,000-Agent Runtime Division

Controlled 1,000-agent supervised division across 10 subdivisions and 100 lanes.

Open Runtime Division

1,777-Department Runtime Map

Map all 1,777 departments to supervised runtime readiness and lane coverage.

Open Department Runtime Map

Department-Gated Runtime Expansion

Approve department gates and activate supervised runtime capacity by department.

Open Department-Gated Runtime Expansion

5,000-Agent Runtime Corps

Scale supervised runtime capacity up to a 5,000-agent cap through approved department gates only.

Open 5,000-Agent Runtime Corps

20,000-Agent Runtime Army

Scale supervised runtime capacity toward a 20,000-agent cap through approved department gates.

Open 20,000-Agent Runtime Army

Full 47,979-Agent Runtime Fleet

Scale to the full registered Agent Command Center fleet through approved department gates.

Open Full 47,979-Agent Runtime Fleet

Advanced control plane review

Full-Fleet Load Test

Stress-test the 47,979-agent control system with simulated load and failure scenarios.

Open Full-Fleet Load Test

Full-Fleet Observability Command Wall

One command-wall view for fleet scale, department gates, stages, cohorts, and audit health.

Open Observability Command Wall

Role-aware executive view for reviewing safety boundaries, observability, and next decisions.

Enterprise Pilot Room

A pilot-ready review room with scope, stakeholder roles, and go/no-go decision support.

Enterprise Pilot Packet Exporter

Generate a shareable pilot packet with executive summary and safety boundaries.

Enterprise Pilot Packet Exporter

Continual Harness Operator Mode

Active operator integration with explicit permission scopes, allowlisted operations, and audit logging.

Open Continual Harness Operator Mode

Validator Map

View Validator Map

Review / Scorecard

Open the decision page for the stakeholder demo and review readiness.

View Review Materials

Legal / Copyright

Review legal boundaries, IP ownership, and data privacy models.

Legal / Copyright

What this is / what this is not

Plain-English boundary setting

What this is

  • A stakeholder-facing enterprise review portal.
  • A command-center architecture presentation.
  • A registry, runtime-gating, safety-boundary, observability, and pilot-review surface.
  • A safe simulator and controlled review environment.
  • A proof of large-scale AI operations architecture, validator-driven QA, runtime-boundary design, and enterprise communication.

What this is not

  • Not raw activate-all.
  • Not unrestricted live automation.
  • Not 47,979 autonomous agents running freely.
  • Not arbitrary shell execution.
  • Not arbitrary SQL.
  • Not deploy execution.
  • Not rollback execution.
  • Not alert sending.
  • Not a grant of runtime permission.
  • Not a replacement for enterprise authorization, legal review, security review, or scoped pilot approval.

For Recruiters / Hiring Managers

Why this matters

What this project proves

  • AI-assisted software development at scale
  • Full-stack system architecture with Supabase / Netlify
  • Runtime safety and permission design (Zero-Live-Agents default)
  • Validator-driven QA and automated safety enforcement
  • Enterprise-ready stakeholder review surfaces
  • Technical communication for complex AI operations
  • Large-scale registry and hierarchy modeling
  • Product-led demo and stakeholder review design

The value is not that 47,979 agents are running freely. The value is that the command structure, safety model, runtime gates, observability surfaces, pilot materials, and packet exporter are visible before any unrestricted runtime is considered.

Glossary

Key system terms

MVP-50

Historical stakeholder demo verification milestone.

MVP-68

Current public enterprise review milestone: Enterprise Pilot Packet Exporter.

Registered agent

A registered command-center operating unit listed in the static registry.

Live runtime agent

A controlled runtime process or runtime capacity record. Not equivalent to unrestricted autonomous execution.

Runtime activation

A gated, staged, audited process. Not raw activate-all.

Enterprise Pilot Packet

A shareable review package containing executive summary, safety boundaries, success criteria, risks, legal links, and decision support.

Raw activate-all

Explicitly disabled.

© 2026 Devin O’Rourke. All rights reserved.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a public stakeholder review surface. Some pages are static demonstrations, and some pages may be controlled backend-wired status, audit, readiness, approval, or export surfaces. No page enables raw activate-all, arbitrary shell execution, arbitrary SQL, command execution, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or unrestricted automation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
